questions.text: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: A plane mirror always forms a virtual, erect image of the same size as the object, located the same distance behind the mirror as the object is in front.

questions.text: Which type of lens is used to correct myopia (short-sightedness)?
questions.explanation: Myopia is corrected using a concave (diverging) lens that pushes the focal point of the eye back onto the retina.

questions.text: When a ray of light passes from water (denser medium) into air (rarer medium), the angle of refraction is:

options.text: less than the angle of incidence
equal to the angle of incidence
greater than the angle of incidence

questions.explanation: When light moves from a denser to a rarer medium it bends away from the normal, so the angle of refraction is greater than the angle of incidence.

questions.text: The refractive index of a medium is given by $n = c/v$, where $c$ is the speed of light in vacuum. If the speed of light in a medium is $2 \times 10^{8}$ m/s, what is its refractive index?
An object is placed 10 cm in front of a concave mirror of focal length 20 cm. The image formed is:
questions.explanation: When the object lies between the pole and focus of a concave mirror (here $u = 10$ cm, $f = 20$ cm), the image is virtual, erect, and magnified — formed behind the mirror.

questions.text: The power of a lens is $+2.5$ D. Its focal length is:
questions.explanation: Power $P = 1/f$ with $f$ in metres. So $f = 1/P = 1/2.5 = 0.4$ m $= 40$ cm. The positive sign indicates a convex lens.

questions.text: The refractive index of glass is 1.5. The approximate speed of light in glass is:
Identify the type of mirror shown in the ray diagram below.
questions.explanation: The diagram shows parallel incident rays converging at a single focal point after reflection — characteristic of a concave (converging) mirror.

questions.text: Which of the following correctly describes refraction of light when entering a denser medium?

options.text: Bends towards the normal — see diagram
Reflects back into the original medium

questions.explanation: When light enters a denser medium, its speed decreases and it bends towards the normal at the interface.

questions.text: An object 4 cm tall is placed 20 cm in front of a convex lens of focal length 10 cm. The image is:

options.text: real, inverted, 4 cm tall, at 20 cm on the other side
virtual, erect, 4 cm tall, at 20 cm on the same side
real, inverted, 8 cm tall, at 40 cm on the other side
real, erect, 2 cm tall, at 10 cm on the other side

questions.explanation: Lens formula $1/v - 1/u = 1/f$ with $u = -20$, $f = +10$ gives $1/v = 1/10 + 1/(-20) = 1/20$, so $v = +20$ cm. Magnification $m = v/u = 20/(-20) = -1$ — image is real, inverted, same size (4 cm), at 20 cm on the opposite side.

questions.text: A concave mirror produces a real image three times the size of an object. If the focal length is 20 cm, the object distance $u$ is approximately:
questions.explanation: For a real, inverted, magnified image, $m = -v/u = -3$, so $v = 3u$. Substituting into $1/v + 1/u = 1/f$ with $f = -20$ cm: $4/(3u) = -1/20$, so $u = -80/3 \approx -26.7$ cm.
